criteria_results.criterion: Domain is dependency-free apart from runtime framework basics
criteria_results.evidence: Domain csproj has no package, framework, or project references; source search found no higher-layer or framework refs.
Application csproj references only WoongBlog.Domain.

criteria_results.criterion: Infrastructure depends on Application + Domain only
criteria_results.evidence: Infrastructure csproj references Application and Domain only.
Api csproj references Application and Infrastructure only.
Project graph is acyclic.
No project reference and no Infrastructure namespace matches under Application.
No higher-layer namespace matches under Domain.

criteria_results.criterion: Application must not expose ASP.NET HTTP result abstractions
criteria_results.evidence: No ASP.NET HTTP result/source tokens under Application; ArchitectureTests include reflected type-surface checks.
No service scope or service locator tokens found under Application.
AiHttpResultMapper and WorkVideoEndpoints map application results to IResult in Api.

criteria_results.criterion: DbContext and concrete stores live in Infrastructure
criteria_results.evidence: WoongBlogDbContext and concrete *Store implementations are under WoongBlog.Infrastructure; production source search found no DbContext usage outside Infrastructure.

criteria_results.criterion: Handlers and abstraction interfaces live in Application
criteria_results.evidence: MediatR handlers and primary store/port abstractions live in Application; Infrastructure has a framework-facing identity interaction interface with Application naming.
Uses AiActionStatus enum only; HTTP mapping is in Api.
Uses WorkVideoResultStatus enum only; HTTP mapping is in Api.
Handlers/interfaces are in Application, concrete persistence stores in Infrastructure, endpoints in Api.
Commands/queries/policies/contracts are in Application; provider/background/store implementations are in Infrastructure.
Handlers/ports/results are in Application; storage/HLS/transcoder/cleanup implementations are in Infrastructure.
AddApplication and AddInfrastructure exist; Api module extensions still register concrete Infrastructure adapters.
DbContext and design-time factory are in Infrastructure; migrations assembly points to Infrastructure; runtime bootstrap remains schema-patch based.
Projects are split, but UnitTests references Infrastructure/ASP.NET and contains component-style tests.

yellow_flags.why_it_matters: Split assemblies still use WoongBlog.Api.* namespaces, weakening readability and grep-based ownership checks.
yellow_flags.suggested_cleanup: Normalize namespaces in a dedicated low-risk PR or document the convention.

yellow_flags.area: Api composition knows concrete Infrastructure adapters
yellow_flags.why_it_matters: Api module registration extensions import and register concrete Infrastructure types.
yellow_flags.suggested_cleanup: Move module infrastructure registrations behind Infrastructure-owned helpers later.

yellow_flags.area: DbContext search synchronization depends on Application support helpers
yellow_flags.why_it_matters: Persistence uses Application support helpers during SaveChanges.
yellow_flags.suggested_cleanup: Move search helper ownership to Domain or Infrastructure if persistence owns indexing long-term.

yellow_flags.area: Infrastructure-shaped AI options live in Application
yellow_flags.why_it_matters: Provider secrets/runtime settings blur option ownership.
yellow_flags.suggested_cleanup: Split Application runtime policy options from Infrastructure provider options later.

yellow_flags.why_it_matters: Infrastructure is host infrastructure rather than pure non-HTTP infrastructure.
yellow_flags.suggested_cleanup: Defer unless an Api-only HTTP boundary is desired.

yellow_flags.area: EF migration workflow remains bootstrap/schema-patch based
yellow_flags.why_it_matters: Design-time factory exists, but no checked-in migrations folder is present.
yellow_flags.suggested_cleanup: Decide separately whether EF migrations should become the deployment workflow.

yellow_flags.area: Unit test project includes component/infrastructure tests
yellow_flags.why_it_matters: These tests use Infrastructure, EF InMemory, ASP.NET contexts, filesystem/process fakes, or HttpClient.
yellow_flags.suggested_cleanup: Move or relabel during the next test-rebalancing PR.

yellow_flags.why_it_matters: Infrastructure code uses an Application namespace segment, which can confuse boundary scans.
yellow_flags.suggested_cleanup: Rename/repath to Infrastructure identity interaction naming later.

deferred_items: Namespace normalization from WoongBlog.Api.* to assembly-aligned namespaces.
Moving ContentSearchText/AdminContentJson if search indexing ownership moves to Domain or Infrastructure.
Splitting AI configuration into Application runtime policy options and Infrastructure provider options.
Deciding whether ASP.NET middleware/auth/validation filters should remain in Infrastructure or move closer to Api.
Reclassifying component-style tests currently under WoongBlog.Api.UnitTests.
